title: Cross-SDK worker protocol conformance suite
purpose: Prove the three SDKs are interchangeable. A language-agnostic scenario set defines the protocol behaviours every worker must exhibit; a fake worker-endpoint harness drives those scenarios; each SDK is run against it and must produce identical observable wire behaviour. This is the guarantee that catches drift between the Rust reference and its Python and TS ports.
task: Define the conformance scenarios (scenarios.json) covering register, complete, fail-retryable, fail-terminal, heartbeat, cancellation, reconnect-and-re-report, and backpressure. Build a fake worker-endpoint harness (fake_engine/) that plays each scenario against a connected worker and records the worker's observable behaviour. Wire a thin per-SDK runner (a small worker built with each SDK serving fixed conformance activities) and assert all three produce the same outcomes. Add JSON payload round-trip parity checks across the three languages.

requirements.title: Language-agnostic scenario set
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define the protocol-conformance scenarios as language-agnostic data: register, receive+complete, receive+fail-retryable, receive+fail-terminal, heartbeat, cancellation, reconnect-and-re-report, and backpressure under full concurrency.
acceptance: scenarios.json enumerates each scenario with its inputs (tasks to dispatch, signals to send) and its expected observable worker behaviour (reports emitted, classification, re-reports, peak concurrency).
The expected behaviour for fail-retryable vs fail-terminal is distinct and asserts the wire classification.
The reconnect scenario specifies a disconnect-before-ack and the expected re-report of the un-acked result before any new task.
The backpressure scenario specifies a max-concurrency of 2 with 5 tasks and an expected peak concurrency of 2.
A README documents how a new SDK is added to the suite.

requirements.title: Fake worker-endpoint harness
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a fake worker-endpoint harness that speaks the aion-proto worker protocol, plays a scenario against a connected worker, and records the worker's observable behaviour for assertion.
acceptance: fake_engine/ implements the worker-endpoint side of the aion-proto protocol enough to run every scenario (handshake, register, dispatch tasks, accept results/failures/heartbeats, simulate cancellation and disconnect).
The harness records, per scenario, the registration set, the result/failure reports with classification, heartbeats received, re-reports after reconnect, and observed peak concurrency.
The harness can drive a worker process regardless of its implementation language (it talks the wire, not an SDK API).
A self-test runs the harness against the Rust SDK conformance runner and asserts every scenario's recorded behaviour matches scenarios.json.

requirements.title: Per-SDK runners and three-way equivalence
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL run each of the three SDKs (Rust, Python, TypeScript) against the harness and SHALL assert all three produce identical observable behaviour for every scenario.
acceptance: A thin conformance worker is built with each SDK, registering the same fixed conformance activities (an echo activity, a retryable-fail activity, a terminal-fail activity, a heartbeating slow activity, a cancellation-observing activity).

The suite runs all three workers through every scenario in scenarios.json and asserts the recorded behaviour is identical across languages (same reports, same classification, same re-report on reconnect, same peak concurrency).
A drift in any one SDK (e.g. a mis-classified failure or a missing re-report) fails the suite with a clear per-scenario, per-language diff.
The suite runs without a live engine — only the fake harness — and is gated to skip cleanly if an SDK's toolchain is unavailable in the environment (logged skip, per CO6).

requirements.title: JSON payload round-trip parity
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL verify that the same input value encodes to an equivalent JSON Payload and decodes back to an equal value in each of the three SDKs.
acceptance: A fixed set of representative values (nested object, array, numbers, booleans, null, unicode string) is encoded and decoded by each SDK's codec.
The encoded Payload content-type tag is the JSON baseline in all three (CO7), and the byte/string form is equivalent modulo insignificant whitespace.
Decoding the other SDK's encoding yields an equal value (Rust decodes Python's encoding, etc.), proving cross-language interchange.
The parity check fails if any SDK alters the content-type tag or loses a field on round-trip.

boundaries: SHALL NOT exercise a real aion-server or engine — the harness is a fake worker-endpoint per CO6.
SHALL NOT modify the SDKs to pass — a conformance failure is a bug fixed in the offending SDK's brief, not patched here.
SHALL NOT define new protocol behaviour — it asserts the behaviour AR-001..AR-010 implement against the AW contract.
verification: The conformance suite runs all three SDKs against the fake harness and passes every scenario.
JSON payload round-trip parity passes across all three SDKs, including cross-language decode.
A deliberately-broken SDK build (e.g. flipping a classification) is shown to fail the suite (negative check documented).
Each SDK's own toolchain gates still pass (Rust clippy/fmt/test; Python ruff/mypy/pytest; TS tsc/eslint/vitest).
No lint-bypass directives in any language (CO2).
